text_blocks.text: The mascot of Northwestern Athletics is Willie the
Wildcat. The first mascot, however, was a live, caged bear
cub from the Lincoln Park Zoo named Furpaw who was
brought to the playing field on the day of a game to greet
the fans. But after a losing season, the team, deciding that
Furpaw was to blame for its misfortune, banished him from
campus forever. Willie the Wildcat made his debut in 1933
first as a logo, and then in three dimensions in 1947, when
members of the Alpha Delta fraternity dressed as wildcats
during a Homecoming Parade. The Northwestern University
Marching Band (NUMB) performs at all home football games
and leads cheers in the student section and performs the Alma
Mater at the end of the game.
